title: Web3 Staking - Open Lido App
meta_description: web3 staking through Lido opens the official app to stake ETH, receive stETH, wrap to wstETH, check rewards, or withdraw.
hero_h2: Stake ETH and keep liquidity with stETH
hero_lead: web3 staking with Lido routes ETH holders to the official app to stake, receive stETH, review rewards, wrap to wstETH, or withdraw from the protocol.
what_is: Lido is a liquid staking protocol centered on Ethereum, where users can deposit ETH and receive stETH as a transferable tokenized position. The protocol mechanics are documented in the Lido token docs.

how.d: Open the official Lido app and connect a supported Ethereum wallet.
Enter the amount of ETH to stake and review the wallet transaction before signing.
After staking, stETH represents the ETH supplied through the Lido protocol and can be held, transferred, or wrapped.
Use the app to review rewards, wrap to wstETH, or start a withdrawal when needed.

points.p: Staking ETH through Lido mints stETH, which represents ETH supplied to the protocol while remaining usable across supported DeFi venues.
Core deployments are listed in the Lido deployed contracts documentation for domain and address checks.
Lido operates around Ethereum proof-of-stake; the base staking model is explained in the Ethereum staking guide.

faq.q: What is web3 staking on Lido?
faq.a: It is an app route for ETH holders to use Lido's liquid staking protocol and receive stETH after staking ETH through the official interface.

faq.q: Is web3 staking with Lido custodial?
faq.a: Lido describes its protocol flow as non-custodial, but users still interact with smart contracts and should verify the domain, wallet permissions, and transaction details.

faq.q: What token do I receive from web3 staking?
faq.a: Staking ETH through Lido returns stETH; users may also wrap stETH into wstETH for integrations that prefer static balances.

faq.q: Can I withdraw after web3 staking?
faq.a: The Lido app includes a withdrawals flow; withdrawing burns stETH and follows Lido and Ethereum withdrawal queues, so timing can vary.

faq.q: How is web3 staking different from running a validator?
faq.a: Running an Ethereum validator requires validator setup and operations; Lido batches user deposits through protocol validators and issues stETH to users.

footer_note: This page is not the official Lido app; verify stake.lido.fi before connecting a wallet, and treat this as educational, not financial advice.
